questions.question: Two concentric circles are of radii $5 \text{ cm}$ and $3 \text{ cm}$. Find the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle.

steps: Let $O$ be the center, $AB$ be the chord of the larger circle tangent to the smaller circle at $P$.
Apply Pythagoras theorem in $\triangle OPA$ where $OA=5, OP=3$ to find $AP$, then double it.
solution.derivation: AP = \sqrt{OA^2 - OP^2} = \sqrt{5^2 - 3^2} = \sqrt{25-9} = 4 \text{ cm}. \text{ Chord } AB = 2 \times AP = 2 \times 4 = 8 \text{ cm}.

questions.question: If a tangent $PA$ drawn from an external point $P$ to a circle with center $O$ makes an angle of $30^\circ$ with the chord $AB$, where $AB$ is the diameter, find $\angle APB$.

steps: Use the property that the angle between tangent and chord equals the angle in the alternate segment.
In $\triangle PAB$, use the angle sum property or circle properties.
solution.derivation: \angle ABP = 90^\circ (\text{angle in a semicircle}). \text{ Given } \angle PAB = 30^\circ. \text{ In } \triangle PAB, \angle APB = 180^\circ - (90^\circ + 30^\circ) = 60^\circ.

questions.question: In the given figure, if $\angle OAB = 40^\circ$, find $\angle ACB$ where $O$ is the center of the circle.

steps: Identify that $\triangle OAB$ is isosceles with $OA=OB$.
Find $\angle AOB$ and use the theorem that the angle at the center is double the angle at the circumference.

questions.question: A quadrilateral $ABCD$ is drawn to circumscribe a circle. If $AB = 6 \text{ cm}$, $BC = 7 \text{ cm}$ and $CD = 4 \text{ cm}$, find the length of $AD$.

steps: Use the property that sums of opposite sides of a circumscribed quadrilateral are equal.
Substitute the given values into $AB + CD = BC + AD$.
solution.derivation: AB + CD = BC + AD \implies 6 + 4 = 7 + AD \implies 10 = 7 + AD \implies AD = 3 \text{ cm}.

questions.question: Two tangents $TP$ and $TQ$ are drawn to a circle with center $O$ from an external point $T$. If $\angle PTQ = 70^\circ$, find $\angle POQ$.

steps: Use the property that the opposite angles of a quadrilateral formed by two tangents and two radii are supplementary.
solution.derivation: \angle OPT = \angle OQT = 90^\circ. \text{ In quadrilateral } OPTQ, \angle POQ + \angle PTQ = 180^\circ. \angle POQ = 180^\circ - 70^\circ = 110^\circ.
